title: Great coffee/Tea creamer
text: I LOVE this product. It smells good, tastes good, and adds real creaminess. A great alternative to dairy creamers. It also adds some creaminess to my fruit smoothies. I highly recommend.

title: Good instant coffee
text: I have to admit, I had very low expectations of this product due to less than stellar experiences with other instant coffees. I figured that if it's drinkable it will be okay. It's actually very good! It doesn't taste like a fresh cup of french press, but it's pretty tasty and is significantly better than some drip coffees - mellow, not bitter. I bought this to take to work as I don't have access to a coffee pot. The container is huge, but I'll be buying more when I run out.

title: great coffee at a great price.
text: Great coffee at a great price. Shipping was fast as well.

title: Gum with a burst of energy
text: Zenergy gum was great especially if you don't feel like drinking a hot cup of coffee. I love the fact it is sugar free and no aspartame. The gum Zenergy also has vitamin bs in it to add to caffeine. The only thing that could improve is taste its ok could be better but not bad. I all would most definitely recommend easily stick in pocket or purse and when you are feeling a little drowsy chew a piece! Recommended!!

title: If you like coffee, keep looking. Would not recommend.
text:
It works fine in the keurig maker. This blend is listed as "light roast" but should be listed as "browned water".
Would be perfect for those who do not like coffee.
I will say that Green Mountain Dark Magic cups are fantastic, but this "flavored coffee" is one of the worst I have ever had. Now I have a box of disgusting k-cups I will not use. It hardly even produces an aroma. So disappointed given the brand.
Keep looking if you want a bold flavored coffee, this is not it.

title: Yuck. But cheap.
text: These flavors are awful and all very similar. Weak coffee. If you're looking to have a bunch of cups for little cost, you've found it. Can't give this away!

title: Like a cup of cocoa.

title: My favorite
text: I love this coffee,it's got a delightful flavor of truly good coffee. Not strong or too weak, just good coffee...I think I'll order more right now...

title: Good
text: This tea is good.  Could be better in flavor and aroma.  Does not hold up well in a latte.

title: Hard to tell this is instant coffee
text:
I like to hike and camp, and used to log around a French press. In the interest of saving weight in my pack, I tried these, and I have to say this certainly does the trick!

I just fire up the jetboil stove and viola! Coffee in two minutes... just like magic!

title: So happy I found this coffee!
text:
I was going to have to give up coffee all together until I found Puroast coffee. From New Orleans, I've been drinking coffee since I was 4 years old, dark roast coffee and chicory and I know what good coffee should taste and smell like. I tried the Puroast French Roast coffee in desperation and was so happy I did. Beautifully full bodied and rich tasting, sans the chicory of course, but I can live with that. I also tried their Expresso style, and didn't really care for it, but that is just my taste. If that is your style, then just see for yourself. I also get the k-cups for when I am at work.
I would highly recommend this brand. Find the style you like, you won't be disappointed.

title: Only coffee I can drink!
text: I have to be careful with any acidic foods and this coffee fits that bill to the tee.  French Roast is full bodied and tastes wonderful.  Finding this low acid, great tasting coffee, I can keep drinking my favorite beverage without sacrificing any flavor.  Very, very pleased with this coffee.

title: Deep coffee flavor
text: Smells wonderful…real coffee flavor

title: Not entirely our cup of tea
text: Nice ground coffee, not really our taste though. It is a mild version of coffee, probably perfect for parties and stuff where folks don't like their java strong. When it comes to mornings, though, we prefer a stronger blend. Folgers does make a good cup of coffee, just not our taste or preferred blend. Nice value pack.

title: Great way to start the day!
text: I usually have at least one energy drink a day, typically in the morning before I go for my run. I am not a coffee drinker, so I am always in the market for a way to supplement my energy. I really like this Runa Bold as one of my new favorites. I love the tropical fruit flavor and when it's icy cold it's definitely just what I need to get my morning started. I love the boost it gives me. I also enjoy having one in the mid afternoon at work to help me through the remainder of the day. Highly recommend!

title: Great espresso for your Nespresso machine!
text: I love espresso and buy capsules for my Nespresso machine on a regular basis. Of course, I had been using Nespresso capsules and I find them super expensive (of course unless they are running a coupon or sale). This Ilan's brand is a great alternative. Same great taste and strength but at lesser of a cost than the Nespresso brand. I am sold on these and will be using them exclusively going forward. Highly recommend!

title: A little strong, but wonderful!
text: I love Nespresso! This double espresso is just perfect (albeit a bit strong). Full bodied, wonderful flavor and a great way to finish a dinner or just to have as a mid afternoon pick me up. True to other Nespresso blends, this variety is easy to make and just wonderful to savor! Be sure you like your coffee strong with this double variety.

title: An absolutely great variety pack of espressos!
text: I love Nespresso! This variety espresso pack is just perfect. All blends are terrific (although I do favor the medium blend). Full bodied, wonderful flavor and a great way to finish a dinner or just to have as a mid afternoon pick me up. True to other Nespresso blends, this variety pack is pleasing to all pallets and just wonderful to savor!

title: Another great Nespresso product!
text: I love Nespresso! This medium roast espresso is just perfect. Full bodied, wonderful flavor and a great way to finish a dinner or just to have as a mid afternoon pick me up. True to other Nespresso blends, this variety is easy to make and just wonderful to savor!

title: Makes a great sweet coffee blend drink!
text: I wasn't sure what to expect when I got these. I love a good coffee and I also love a great hot chocolate, but German Chocolate Cake? Actually, this was a great blend of both. However, it's not an everyday coffee for me, too overly sweet for morning/breakfast use. This does make, however, a great after dinner blend with dessert or even in the afternoon when you are craving something sweet. I really did like this.

title: My new favorite morning coffee!
text: Great tasting, full bodied, this is terrific blend. Packaged in a convenient 6 pack, this is an economical way to have access to some of the best tasting coffee you will ever have. I highly recommend this coffee for any time of day, not just morning!

title: My most favorite cup of coffee!
text: This is one of my favorite Keurig coffees. The caramel honey Starbucks flavor is full bodied, rich and has an amazing taste. I prefer to have this flavor mid-afternoon and I usually 'supplement' it with some shortbread cookies to give me the energy boost I need. To me, this home brew tastes just as good, if not better, than anything I can get at a Starbucks store and I can have it at home anytime I want it. I am hooked!

title: These are a hit with myself and my entire family!
text: These are absolutely some of my favorites. First, I love Junior mints and buy them for snacks quite often. Then, when you combine it with hot chocolate and K-Cup pods you have one of the most amazing hot drinks on the market today. I love making a cup mid-afternoon as a snack and pick me up. The mint is totally refreshing and the hot chocolate just cannot be beat for flavor. My grandson (age 5) begs for these when he visits. Total hit for my family! Thanks Keurig for another great product!

title: GROSS !!

title: Just average coffee
text: Because I love dark cocoa coffee like Bustelo chocolate, I thought I would try this. It is just average coffee. Will not buy again.

title: Starbucks blah
text: Just not my flavor for coffee. It was bitter, not sweet at all, never distinguished what kind of coffee flavor it was supposed to be.

title: Cadbury Dairy Milk fruit & Nut Chocolate Bar Single bar
text: I am a huge fan of this bar.  Surprisingly good.  I usually purchase the larger, flatter bar.  These are good, but not quite as good as the larger, flatter bars because the flatter bars seem to have more fruit and nuts.  These are smaller, 'single' bars are thicker chocolate but why less fruit and nuts which are key to these chocolate bars. Maybe it's just that the flatter chocolate makes the fruit and nuts stand out? Not sure. In the first 'single' bar I opened I had 2 pieces of raisins (what happened the whole fruit?) and about 3 chips of almond in a 6" chocolate bar. Eh. I am going to go back to the flatter ones.  Just my personal opinion.  The other disappointment is that expiration date on this box is 10/2022.  It's a box of 48.  Should have had at least a year.

title: NOT 100% Kona coffee as advertised
text:
1. Shame on me for not reading the previously posted reviews!
2. NOT KONA COFFEE. it's a blend of anything they'd like
3. Tastes like old regular can coffee
4. Really bummed as I purchased as gifts.
*1 note.. each bag came individually boxed and cello sealed. Like you would like your really delicious 100% KONA coffee to be delivered. Too bad this stuff is junk
5. NO LABEL listing "blend" - one single sticker stating "Aloha gold" yeah. Right.
Grrrrrrrrrrrrr. Always read reviews! After 30 years you'd think I'd have learned.
Cannot recommend this purchase to literally anyone. I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

title: Double Donut pumpkin spice kcups
text: You get 4 boxes of these - so LOTS of kcups and they work in your kcup brewer.  But that's where the positive ends.  You can smell the pumpkin spice faintly - but when brewed it tasted like - watery pumpkin spice! I've never tasted any coffee like it. (FYI - these are DECAF - but that doesn't mean NO coffee)  I actually opened the kcup to see if there were coffee grounds in the cup.  There is some brown stuff that looks like coffee? Not sure.  You should be able to smell or taste coffee period.  No coffee flavor at all - as I said, just watery faint pumpkin spice flavor.  What a huge disappointment.  I landed all 4 in the recycle bin.

title: Kauai Coffee
text: I sent this to a friend of mine who loves her coffee - and hadn't tried this brand yet.  I love this brand.  Any style/flavor.  Rich - of course, it's a bit pricey.

title: Surprisingly Good
text: I've only used it once, but it was surprisingly good. No after taste. I just mixed up a small batch of chocolate cookies without using a recipe. The cookies actually tasted like they had chocolate chips in them. My sister agreed. We were amazed. They were exceptionally good and we couldn't believe they had no chips of bits of chocolate candy. This is a gamechanger in my opinion. I can't wait to play with this product to see what I can produce with it. Exceptional.  No way could I have gotten this with regular sugar. Yes I will be buying more.

title: Not happy, but my sister likes them
text: I should have noticed the fine print. You don't expect to be deceived when buying cookies.  First of all they are advertised as Biscotti.  They are not. They are a hard shortbread type cookie. A biscuit as the package says. Notice the the difference.  They are not sweet. Had there been a little more sweetness they might  have tasted better. They taste like a cross between a cookie and biscuit. They are crumbly and edible, but not really enjoyable. Maybe dipped in a sweet cappuccino will help.  My craving for biscotti continues. Maybe Kroger's can fulfill it. No more surprises. I had to come back to add another star. My sister does like them.  She's not big on sweet things, but likes them dipped in her coffee. To each his own.

Not quite a coffee snob here, but do prefer coffee that is complex, smooth, not too bitter, and just roasty enough. This was none of those things. It almost had a sour flavor, to the point that I double checked the expiration and did a nose test on my creamer to make sure that hadn't expired.

I didn't get any notes of vanilla or macadamia. Made one pot, ended up pouring that out and then tossed the whole bag.

Life is too short to suffer through a bad cup of coffee.

Will definitely keep using this going forward! Only downside is the pump comes kinked in the box with the bottle, which can make pumping a little difficult.

title: Good, with some reservations
text:
If you like Starbucks coffee, you will really enjoy this selection of dark and medium roast coffees. Although a little pricey, this is a good selection of mild to full-flavored coffee blends. I think the key is whether you enjoy Starbucks flavors, which some in our family have found to be a little sharp. If I were buying this assortment, I would want to be aware that some of the flavors are bold, and if that is what you want, you will enjoy them. But if you are looking for a more donut-house flavor coffee, this is not it.

I priced this item in our local store, and the Amazon price is much more reasonable. But you can do even better price-wise with other brands in the supermarket when the k-cups go on sale.

title: Hard to Pump, Not Great Flavor
text: I love the flavors of Coffee Mate creamers but these big bottles do not taste the same as their refrigerated versions. They are of a creamy syrup consistency, which also lacks the sweetness that the refrigerated flavor of the same has. The pumps are also not effective, as one pump takes so much effort due to the skinny, tall design with a minuscule opening for dispensing (and one of the pumps broke upon first use), yet yields only a teeny stream of creamy syrup. The bottle says you only need one pump per cup of coffee but I beg to differ. It takes at least 10 pumps to fill up one tiny creamer container like you get at a restaurant on the table when you order coffee. It's supposed to be concentrated so you use less but the taste is lost in translation. Really great price/value for these huge bottles IF they redesign the pumps AND add a bit more sweetness and MORE CREAM to the creamer.

text: It is very hard to find the sugar-free singles in the stores in our area. I was happy to see that Amazon carries them at a good price. I love the French Vanilla. These make your coffee taste decadent without too many extra calories. The individual containers are great, and they don't need to be refrigerated, making them super-convenient. title: Good Coffee Selections
text:
I like the Italian and Seattle roasts. Both are decently bold flavorful dark roasts that aren't bitter. I drink my coffee black, no sugar so I pay attention to these things. Good coffee.
My mom likes the caramel macchiato on occasion but says its a little odd tasting. Still, she asks for it so, there's that. She really enjoys the Kona blend. Says it reminds her of their vacations to Hawaii. I found it flavorful but a little weaker than I prefer.
My biggest complaint is all four flavors are just higgledy-piggledy thrown in one box. Even dividers would have been nice. It's a mild pain to dig through the box looking for a specific flavour especially when that one, Kona, is being used the most often. But, we enjoy all four flavors to varying degrees.

title: Very Small Containers
text: We're used to the refrigerator version of Coffee Mate and the flavor of the French Vanilla singles was very similar to each other. The regular flavor was okay and the Hazelnut was okay if slightly off. It just wasn't as pleasant as we expected. The biggest problem we encountered with these singles was how small they were. My mother likes her coffee light and sweet and it easily took three singles to get her coffee the way she liked it. It seems like it takes a lot of product to get to a desired outcome.  Nice to have around when there's no power or to take tent or trailer camping.

title: Didn't love it: no real flavor, over-roasted, and seemed stale
text:
*UPDATED REVIEW*
I returned it. This is the worst coffee I have ever had. Seriously. I made another cup today, had a sip and promptly called Amazon to return it. Awful. I wonder if what was in the bag might have been incorrect? This was very dark and sharp/bitter, not smooth at all. I used a normal amount of coffee (not too much), and the result was the same three day sin a row. Dumped the cup I had made, called Amazon and tossed the rest of it out.
---

Had high hopes for this, but found it harsh and unpleasant. The flavor is very one-dimensional, flat and strong. No hints of anything else here, just strong, dark roasted unremarkable coffee in a bag labeled medium roast. I found the roast incongruent with the labeling, definitely overdone for "medium". The lack of any meaningful flavor highlights the harshness of the roast. It just does not have any warmth, no pleasant robust feel or sweetness at all, no real body, just a source of caffeine. Also, it seemed a bit stale - not fresh. Will not be buying this again, and may not finish the bag.

title: Peppermint is light, no bitterness.
text: I love peppermint n lots of circumstances, but especially in coffee. This is a very mild peppermint taste - it's definitely there, but softer than I really like. The coffee smells awesome as it's making. This is a light brew so it's not bitter or terribly strong and that's always good. I had to add sugar and a dash of peppermint flavored creamer to enhance the flavor for my tastes, but this is a good coffee.

title: Soy free! Tastes as good as the coffee shop
text:
I stumbled on the Peppermint Mocha in the grocery store the other day and I had to try it. The peppermint mocha from the coffee shops is delicious, but I am not interested in paying five bucks a cup.

This goes in the fridge and is added cold. I use it with our fancy-schmancy espresso machine and it is amazingly good. I add this cold to my cup first. Then do the steamed milk froth and the espresso double shot. Stir and drink - done over ice makes essentially a frappucino.

There's no weird chemical aftertaste and the Peppermint comes through loud and clear, but doesn't overwhelm. This is already sweet, so no need to add sugar at all.

title: Two of three people like these. Weird chemical aftertaste for me
text:
We have mixed results here for these creamer flavors. Three people in our house this week and we all tried each flavor multiple times - vanilla, hazelnut, and original. My house guest liked all three and especially the hazelnut. My husband felt like the original was a reasonable substitute for real milk in a pinch or when we're down at the ranch in the backcountry.

And then there's me....the oddball. I just did not like any of these. I get a very strong chemical type aftertaste. It reminds me of those nasty fake, chemical sweeteners. The hazelnut was the strongest one with this effect for me - no clue what the "artificial flavorings" actually are, but I can't tolerate them.

title: Tastes like the coffee n the stores. Little bit bitter without milk
text:
We mostly use kcups or beans for the espresso machine, but my husband does like to brew a regular old school pot of coffee when we have company. This medium roast ground coffee is fine for a quick cup, but it's not a delicious concoction like fresh beans from the espresso machine - that's ok.

All three varieties taste more like a dark roast from any other coffee brand instead of a milder, medium roast. There's no burnt flavor, but definitely more bitter than we expected. I add milk and it evens out. Basically, this tastes like what we get from Starbucks when we're traveling.

text: I like it. Nice for making a quick mug of hot cocoa. I make it depending on what I am feeling like. Sometimes I make it with water, sometimes non dairy milk, sometimes I add a splash of vanilla or cayenne pepper, sometimes I boost the choco flavor with a little extra plain cacao. Has a 2024 best by date.

title: smooth, dark, bold- no bitterness or tanginess; all wonderully robust dark flavor
text: I love strong, bold, smooth dark coffee. Double Diamond (and Dark Magic, Krispy-Cream1837) are my favorites for meeting this preference. I like that Double Diamond is strong and smooth made with the smallest water setting, as well as stands up to greater amounts of water without it tasting watered down. While I drink mine black 95% of the time, it does nicely with creamer. Never bitter, never tart; it is a true bold coffee. Love it (and the others mentioned).

title: super strong, dark, wonderful
text: I love Green Mountain Dark Magic, and had been buying that for years, and no other kind. Recently I decided to try Double Diamond. I didn't expect it to impress me, and definitely didn't expect to like it more than Dark Magic. Well, I do. Double Diamond is even darker, bolder, stronger, and tastier than Dark Magic. I like them both, but if I had to choose I'd go with Double Diamond. When I brew a K-cup, I always choose the 10oz and just drink it. With Double Diamond, I did that and while I was loving the super strong taste at that level, I decided to brew in another 6oz of water. It was still deliciously strong! So now I feel like I am getting more bang for my buck since I can make a bigger cup of coffee with one K-cup. I'd say the Double Diamond will make for an excellent iced coffee when the season calls for it. It won't water down too easily. YUM!!!

title: dark, bold, smooth
text: I like very few k-cups across all brands. Green Mountain Dark Magic and Green Mountain Double Diamond are the only two I really like. Both are dark, smooth, and bold. Been drinking both for years, with Dark Magic for the greatest amount of time.

title: Excellent Set of Flavored Coffee Pods
text:
This Winter Wonderland Variety Pack by Victor Allen is a delicious deal on great coffee.

There are 24 pods of each of the following flavors: Sugar Cookie Coffee, Vanilla Butter Cream Coffee, Cinnamon Bun Coffee and Peppermint Bark Coffee. Each of them is unique, rich and brews beautifully. Our favorite has been the Cinnamon Bun but we’ve enjoyed every one of them. The flavors are not overpowering but are present.

We use a Keurig Vue so in order to use K Cups we have to use an adapter. Some K Cups tend to work better than others with the adapter but these have worked with no issue.

At under .42 a pod this set is an outstanding deal that anyone who enjoys these seasonal flavors will love.

I would not hesitate to recommend this set and we will be purchasing more of this brand in the future.

⭐️As with all Drew’s Reviews, if I discover something new about this product - good or bad - I will UPDATE my review. I believe in creating detailed, complete reviews because, like you, I depend on them when I am shopping. If you found my review HELPFUL - please click ⬇️ below!⭐️

text: To be completely honest, I’m not a fan of pikes place roast, I don’t know why, but every time I go into a Starbucks and try it, I never like it. I also get fresh beans from a speciality supplier for my home, but I wanted to be able to bring some cheaper coffee to take with me to work that I wouldn’t mind sharing with others; so the Pikes Place definitely got used by my coworkers. As for the other roasts it was much more my style of coffee. In general I don’t feel like Starbucks coffee is something you savor, it’s more like a quick cup of joe when you need a hit of caffeine during a midday slump; and in that category it definitely gets the job done.

title: Good flavor very satisfying
text: We have a large coffee station in our house, because we love flavored coffee. This is one of the seven flavors we keep in stock. This coffee doesn't taste bitter at all, has great flavor and just in general is satisfying. I like this coffee because it doesn't have too much of a sugary or dessert like flavor, but it does have good flavor to it without it being too overpowering.

title: More cinnamon then sugar, but great taste
text: This has a light taste, but it doesn't quite taste like sugar cookie. In my opinion it tastes more like cinnamon, or a snicker doodle, but the taste overall is really nice and I enjoy it.

title: Just the right about of chocolate flavor, great smell
text: Very good taste, the mocha flavor offsets any bitterness of the coffee. It's not to sweet, great chocolate flavor and smelt really good. I do have to say I have bought several different brands of mocha/chocolate flavored coffee in the past and one of two things happened 1) the chocolate was so weak you couldn't taste it or 2) the chocolate was so over powering that the it tasted more like chocolate milk than mocha coffee. This was the PERFECT middle ground to that problem. It's also very economically priced, and I really enjoy keeping it in stock in my coffee cabinet.

title: Surprisingly Tasty
text: I thought this had a good taste to it, the hazelnut was not overpowering, but still there enough for it to feel like a hazelnut coffee. The coffee itself isn't too bitter, and the hazelnut helps with that as well. Overall a good purchase and I would buy again.

title: Can't really taste the peanut butter
text: One of my husbands all time favorite foods is peanut butter, and since he loves coffee with a sweet flavoring to it, I thought this would be the perfect coffee for him. And while it does smell like peanut butter (the grounds not the coffee once it's brewed), and honestly the coffee was kind of a disappointment because you can barely taste any kind of peanut butter or sweet element to the coffee at all. If you are going to have flavored coffee it should be strong enough for you taste in the average cup of coffee.

title: Surprisingly delicious!
text: I bought this coffee as kind of a joke, I thought to myself, my husband loves coconut and he loves coffee and even though this is going to probably be a horrible combo, it will be super cute to go in the little coffee cans project I was making for his Christmas present. I was so surprised when he tried the coffee out and fell in love with it. It's the perfect combination of sweet with coffee, it's become basically his go to coffee and easily his favorite of the 7 types we stock regularly. If you are on the fence on trying out this flavor, don't be, you have to try it out, it's great.

text: The flavor of this coffee is good. Has a sweet aroma and bold enough flavor to satisfy most palettes. The added caffeine is noticeable and gives you a nice kick. I enjoyed it.

title: Average taste, nothing spectacular but pretty good price.
text:
I'm not a very lucky coffee drinker so I thought the flavor of this was fine. My boyfriend who is a bit pickier said this is a very whatever tasting coffee.

It is a "morning blend" coffee so it's not surprising this coffee doesn't wow or blow minds. Lol

I tried it hot, making it in a French press, and also used it to make cold brew. It's ground a bit fine to make cold brew so it does leave residual coffee particles but it's not bad. I think I prefer it in the French press though.

The price is pretty comparable to bulk pricing so it's not bad at all.

I think this would work well at an office or breakrooms.

text: The pods are expensive, but at least they are recyclable. I love Nespresso coffee I just wish it was cheaper.

title: Yum!  Love this coffee
text: It tastes like maple and it's a nice cup of coffee. Would definitely buy again.  Hard to say much more than that.

title: Not a fan
text: I wanted to "love" my nespresso machine!  I love the machine, but I don't like any of the coffees.  The hazelnut doesn't have any flavor at all---to me.  I'm so disappointed.

title: Coffee
text: The pods were easy to use; they work just like any other coffee pods. The flavor is definitely medium, even with creamer. It does have a distinct flavor that I can't necessarily put my finger on. However I can tell the difference between it and other brands/ favlors. So it has its own flavor. Overall, I liked the flavor and the amount of caffeine can be good for middle of the day when you need a little pick me up... it's not too heavy.

title: Ehhhh....Not great for me but might be okay for you.
text:
This was an okay cup of coffee. It wasn't horrible and it wasn't great. I admit to being something of a coffee snob and I generally grind my beans just before my coffee. This is pre-ground so for me that's a stroke against. I do realize that sometimes pre-ground works better, especially if there are a lot of drinkers so it may be a plus for someone else.

This is a rather mild coffee and I generally prefer a darker roast and a more robust flavor of coffee so this was a little light for me. It was still drinkable so I decided to serve it to some friends. None of them complained about the coffee and several went back for seconds.

If this is your coffee style, it's probably good. If you prefer more robust coffee, this won't be good. I'll serve it up to friends when I have a group over, but for me I'm going back to my hand ground beans.

title: Individual creamers with a long shelf life.
text: What's not to love? These are just like you get in restaurants and have a great shelf life. The flavors are great if you like to add a hint of flavor and they lighten your coffee. I saw several reviews that complained about opened packages, but mine came fine, the heat didn't affect them and they all seem fine. Great for grab and go. I generally drink black coffee at home but a lot of times when I'm out and the coffee isn't "quite as good as at home" (coffee snob here) they are great to have as a backup. I do prefer the Hazelnut and Plain but it's easy to share the French Vanilla with people--they LOVE them!

title: average coffee
text:
The Corner One Organic Signature Roast is a drinkable, but not memorable coffee. I always use the strong setting on my Keurig as I love a deep and rich coffee, but it did not seem to do much for this. I can't even imagine how mild it is on the regular setting and it is definitely not medium-dark as described. It has a bit of a flat flavor with a touch of acidity and reminded me of the coffee I have had in various diners over the years.
The price was very good and it shipped prime and arrived quickly so those are a couple of positives, as is the fact that it is Fair Trade and Organic. What is not so great is that I now have to go through this whole 100 count box of mediocre coffee. It’s going to take awhile.

title: Works Well in Baking - Sugar-Free Chocolate Chip Cookies!
text:
This is my first time using purecane and I'd say it is about 80% as sweet as regular sugar when I use it cup for cup in my chocolate chip cookie recipe. I notice the cookies come out softer and less browned than with regular sugar but they still taste good.

The purecane straight from the bag is a little "cool" to the taste but in the cookies I don't notice that as much. This sweetener blends into butter fairly well. I used Lily's semi-sweet chocolate chips so the cookies were totally sugar free!

The friend I made these cookies for says they have grown on him and I think he wants me to make him some more. He has issues with eating sugar that involve his teeth hurting so I was trying to find a way for him still to enjoy cookies and I think I've hit upon the formula to help him out. I thought the cookies got better the longer they sat in the plastic bag I had them in. They did not get soft, but rather ended up more like shortbread. I kept a few cookies I could not fit in the box. I enjoyed eating them too. :) It looks like I'll be getting this sugar substitute again!

~The Rebecca Review

title: A good one
text: End it up being a very fine addition to my mix. I had purchased a couple of coffee beans that turned out a little on the bitter side, not bad in taste, just bitter, and as I have done in the past I looked for the type of coffee that could turn the tide smooth, and pleasing to the taste. I have to say that this Kauai Coffee Vanilla Macadamia Nut Ground 10 Oz, did the job rather well, although a little pricey it delivered a fine taste. Now, with this popurri of flavors we have a very tasty, a little on the strong side but just enough, combination that makes the ritual of drinking a cup or two a total win situation, for it seems to run out almost immediately upon brewing, and that can not be a bad thing as long as I get at least one cup of each batch. 4 Stars.

title: One out of four.
text:
If you drink your coffee black, fine, you may love ALL of these flavors. I love fall flavors, so I was very excited.

Cinnamon Roll is delightful. Truly. Cream and sugar make the perfect complement.
Apple Crumb is DREADFUL. Cream does NOT go with this flavor at all, in any capacity. The apple is what I was most excited for, and that was a mistake. It's like drinking hot earthy apple juice. Nothing redeemable.
Pumpkin Spice is too spicy. Sugar makes it a bit better, but you'd have to LOAD it, to lose the some spice. Pumpkin spice is supposed to have a hint of spice, not taste like 90% nutmeg. Gross.
Pecan pie (or whatever it's called) is too bitter. Sugar helps a bit, but again, if you don't want a quarter cup of sugar in eight ounces of coffee.... just gross.

The cinnamon roll flavor gets three stars on its own, the rest are garbage. You might like the pecan if you like BITTER, and you might like the pumpkin spice if you want it to burn. I don't know WHO would like the apple, but to each their own.

title: Unhealthy ingredients
text:
I had some of this recently at a friend's house. The taste is moderately good, but it has several questionable ingredients in it which are quite unhealthy: hydrogenated fat (which is soon going to be outlawed and most processed food doesn't contain it anymore), corn syrup, and the artificial sweetener sucralose.

I personally prefer making my own cocoa using [[ASIN:B001VNFHLC organic cocoa powder]], [[ASIN:B00B8YQI58 stevia]], vanilla extract, and [[ASIN:B004LL5DC4 almond milk]]. It's dairy-free, very low calorie, and tastes delicious--without any of the questionable ingredients of packaged cocoa like this.

Ingredients: Sugar, corn syrup solids, vegetable oil (partially hydrogenated coconut or palm kernel and hydrogenated soybean), dairy product solids (from milk), cocoa processed with alkali, and less than 2% of salt, cellulose gum, sodium caseinate, dipotassium phosphate, sodium citrate, sodium aluminosilicate, mono- and diglycerides, guar gum, artificial flavors, sucralose. Made on equipment that also processes soy and wheat.

title: Standard light roast
text:
We enjoy this light roast with cream and sugar for our regular morning cup of coffee. It's like old school diner coffee, a standard serving of coffee with no frills. The aroma ice nice, it has a little bit of a caramel/toffee note to it when you smell it. It's low in acidity while it still has plenty of flavor.

Cons:
-Not single origin, comes from 25 different places
-Not organic
-Not shade grown

So all in all, it's a good roast, but it's not doing anything beneficial for the environment with how they are made and their are coffees for a similar price point that incorporate one of the above practices.

title: Peppermint Bark certainly isn't all bark, no bite!
text:
I got this peppermint bark coffee to review, and I'm glad it's in k-cup form. I don't use flavored coffee in my regular maker because I worry about the oils making a mess in the machine and tainting my unflavored roasts. So enjoying a cup of flavored coffee is definitely a seasonal treat. And the peppermint bark k-cup fits the bill for a holiday coffee.

It's a light roast underneath the peppermint flavoring, but the flavoring makes it seem bold. It tastes like white chocolate and mint. It smells great, very sweet and minty, and a little bit of sugar and cream really brings out the flavor.

I wouldn't drink this every day because I'm not a flavored person, but on the weekends or as a festive way to mix it up around Christmas I would drink it. It makes a good iced coffee too, especially if you use chocolate almond milk for a creamer!

title: A nice medium body roast
text:
This is slightly different than the regular donut shop blend, roasted a little darker. It's still a medium roast, just slightly more bolder. Because you NEED a little bit more bold to get through the holiday season!

We use these K-Cups fairly frequently and the flavor is good, it's a standard cup of coffee that isn't too bland or too over powering. We both used these to make 12 ounce cups of coffee in the mornings. We have had some issues with grounds coming out when we use them which makes a mess. I tried them in my Keurig at work and it happened there too, so it's a cup issue. Didn't happen too much though.

title: Our Favorite
text:
My family has been buying the Don Pablo light roast for a couple of years now on Subscribe and Save. We used to buy the national brand with the regular cup controversies, but we weren't fans. It was burnt. The franchise drove out local shops. It was super expensive. We tried a lot of organic brands. Many do not have light roasts. Many taste earthier than we like. I'm telling you this, because if you like flame-broiled and/or earthy coffees, this might not be the coffee for you.

But we like it. It's not the lightest of light roasts, but it is smooth and carmel-y and very consistent. We've tried a couple of other brands, just as palate cleansers, over the past couple of years and couldn't get back to this one fast enough. We make it in a twenty dollar french press with a seventy buck burr grinder and a water boiler that has a 195 degree setting. If your tastes and budget are fancier than that, you may not love this coffee as much as I do. I don't want to present myself as some kind of expert.

But if you are looking for a decent, affordable organic light roast that is of consistent quality and reliable availability, this is an excellent choice.

Print is pretty.  Medium weight.  Does not feel cheap.  Actually impressed.  Coffee included is good.  Have tried some donut shop duos before.  A good coffee brand.  Not bitter.  Good flavor compared to other flavored coffee.  Kcups do not clog our machine.  I would not hesitate to gift this set (or keep it for myself).  Totally no regrets.

title: winner!
text: Not much for flavored coffee, but we really liked this one.  Tastes best with a splash of heavy cream which really seems to bring out the flavors.  Doesn't taste synthetic like some do.  Really just taste good.  Hubs approved too and he is not much for flavored coffees either.  Doesn't clog the Keurig.  Another plus!  Easy to brew and great taste.  No regrets!

title: Way Less Calories
text:
Iced coffee is a refreshing pick me up, and this one has a vanilla and caramel flavor and zero sugar, unlike most other iced coffees. You can add sugar if you like of course, but I don't think it needs it. The natural sweetness of the flavorings give it a sweetness all its own.
In my opinion, it does have a slight twinge of an artificial flavor, but I find that to often be the case whenever a coffee is additionally flavored.
I have both a Keurig, and a non Keurig K-Cup machine, and these pods fit both. Since one of them doesn't accommodate a large cup, I just brew it in a regular mug and then pour it over ice.
Even with the coffee being hot, it doesn't completely water down as the ice melts when you first pour it over. I do like to wait a minute before pouring it. It definitely stands up to milk or cream.

Cheaper and way less calories than any coffee shop or drive-thru iced coffee.

title: Jolt without jitters
text:
While this might be a medium roast, it is robust and will definitely get you started in the morning.
None of the bitterness or burnt taste that some find displeasing.
Ever notice how different coffees give a different type of 'buzz'? Everyone is different, but for me, when I really need a jolt but can't afford the jitters, this particular one is my go-to.

title: Good flavor
text: Has nice flavor, easy to make and clean up.

title: Great cup of coffee
text:
As a dark roast biased coffee drinker, there's a chance that I lost touch with the flavor of light roast. I think I did!

This Green Mountain light roast is excellent. Well worth the change-up for the dark roast drinker.

title: I’ve not tasted one that I don’t like.
text:
The reason for selecting the assortment is to satisfy the seeming insatiable demand for variety in my coffee klatches. Flavored coffee is not particularly my taste, but it surely is among others. A second reason is that the kidz drink coffee at an age much earlier than I ever had my first cup. The kidz go for the flavored tastes if for no other reason than to tell friends what they’ve tried, liked and don’t like.

For now, the variety is entertaining. There will inevitably be a ‘least liked’ that will remain to evidence what not to buy in the future. How often would you discover that friends will say “How did you know I like ‘Toasted Graham’!”

The packaged variety works for others if not yourself.

title: Rich and Flavorful. Tastes Yummy!
text:
I really like this Donut Shop Vanilla Cream Puff Coffee. It has a really Yummy flavor but it's not overpowering.
Taste like a little bit of heaven and I find that I'm not using as much sugar since it's already sweet.
Definitely a Must Try flavor!

title: STARBUCKS VARIETY PACK IS THE SPICE OF LIFE...

Most of us LOVE our Keurigs! Which means we like our: Fast, semi-expensive coffee made to order right in our own homes.
What's better to put in your beloved Keurig than STARBUCKS??? AND...At less than $.80 a cup...That's a BIG $3+ savings per cup over the in-store price and there's something for everyone in this box.
Between 4 great flavors like:: Pike's Place, House Blend, Breakfast Blend and Columbia - it's hard to choose.

title: Smooth flavor
text:
I was so excited for the chance to review these coffee pods!

Of course, they fit my Kurieg perfectly. They are so aromatic too. You can immediately smell the wonderful coffee scent throughout my kitchen as soon as the lid is popped.

The flavor is so smooth and wonderful. It is the perfect Italian blend. Great price for the product :)

title: Disappointed

title: Best flavor and consistent quality!
text: Such a wonderful aroma and NEVER disappoints. No chemical flavor, no residue or mess at bottom of cup...perfect to the last drop! No bad kcups and the taste is so rich and satisfying!

title: Robust
text: Yes I am one of those people who must have coffee even on hot humid days. These k-cups make it easy to make a decent iced coffee using any keurig brewer. I have a keurig2.0. It does not have an iced option. Nonetheless this made a decent cup of iced coffee. The dark roast is robust and can be tempered with cream and or sweetener. Not quite as smooth as some of the cold brews it still makes a satisfying cold, caffeinated drink.

title: Wonderful Seasonal Brew
text: Who doesn't love peppermint bark? This blend plays homage to the holiday favourite. It is a light roast brightened with a touch of peppermint. the peppermint comes through but does not overwhelm. peppermint can be an assertive flavour but here it serves as a tasty accent. I was unable to detect any white chocolate, however.  It is not sweet; you can add your own sweetener and creamer if desired often avoid peppermint beverages. The ppermint usually takes over. but this one is nicely balanced and the mint is mild. It is a fun rift on a holiday treat and makes a nice seasonal brew.

title: Satisfying
text: This is a delicious medium roast coffee. the pods work perfectly with my Keurig. I liked that the coffee is organic and air trade. It does hae pleasing smoothness and some body.  It is a coffee that mixes well with others. It works well with creamers and milk (dairy and otherwise) as well as with syrups, sugar free and otherwise. It provides a classic base that is neighter too strong nor too weak. It is very reasonably priced for K cups and makes a satisfying cup of joe.

title: Just don't! Buy from the store or Keurig.
text:
This was the second time I purchased this product, and I am disappointed by this second delivery.

This is a set of 4 boxes, with 24 coffee pods. I am not sure what happened this time, but every box has 1 or 2 pods that caused problems. They had to have been stored in a way that the temperature must have gotten too warm and then cooled quickly because I noticed many of the foil seals were bubbled up and tight to the touch, or flattened. They then would sometimes break open on the side when placed in machine. This made the water come out of the pod from the top and spill out coffee grounds. Now, I have to puncture the pods, then make sure it didn't break the seal before I run the machine to make coffee.
I WILL NOT purchase these through Amazon again, thank you for the inconvenience!

[EDIT 3.30.21] This morning was not the morning to mess with my sanity! Even after checking that the pod did not open, it STILL burst open! What the actual * ?! (Picture added this time.)

title: Smell/ flavors are overwhelmingly artificial
text:
I enjoy coffee of any sort, including regular and flavored ones. Not these pods.
After reading such good reviews I was super looking forward to trying them.
My first one was caramel. Chemical taste was so terrible that I dumped after the first sip and decide to chose hazelnut. I felt the same artificial -chemical taste. I reluctantly moved on to the rest of two, just to confirm they were as bad...um um, yes the same. Really? I ended up so many!! At that time I knew I wasted my $, but blew and quickly rinsed my mouth ( but lingered for a while).
I know this sounds like a awfully mean review, and I wondered for weeks of whether to submit or not.
If I am this unhappy about what I bought I'd definitely return, but who wants opened box of coffee?
96 pods remaining. I will donate to local church or something with warning.

PS: those pods have very cute design of colorful seals that I love!
Good job on that part!

title: GREAT STRONG, BOLD DARK COFFEE, ITS OUR FAVORITE!
text: This is the favorite coffee of both my husband and I, as we love Strong Bold coffee, especially in the morning. It's the best STRONG coffee by far of the many coffees we've tried, and that's a lot of different types of coffee. I receive this on auto ship from Keurig every 6 weeks, but yet run out before the next shipment, so being a Prime member, I can order it from Amazon and receive it in 2 days which I absolutely love. I like all different types of coffee, but when we first wakeup this is our go to coffee. Its strong with a great taste without tasting like old burnt, bitter coffee with a bad after taste. If you're a fan of a Strong Bold Dark coffee, then I would definitely recommend this as your first cup of Joe. I enjoy drinking coffee late into the morning and sometimes in the evening.  If you don't like Strong Dark coffee then I would say try something different. I will continue to order this unless they do away with it!

Would recommend for anyone who loves a good strong cup of coffee!

title: Great My Go To Morning Coffee
text:
I really love Bold, dark coffee and this is one of my favorites. I like the boldness, and smooth flavor, its not bitter, nor does it taste like burnt coffee. It's fabulous and a great way to start the day. Its my go to coffee!
I was impressed by the price, as I usually buy from GMC or Keurig websites. I was almost out so found it here and had it in 2 days, rather than a week or so. I'll probably order from Amazon from now on if the price is better, which it was in this case.
The box delivered on my porch said Keurig, and inside were the 4 boxes of 24 count Kcups. I've bought other flavored coffee that just cane loose in one box of 96 .I wasn't too pleased, as there were no date to check for freshness.
Overall, I'm very happy with my purchase and will order again. Although I'm on auto-delivery with Green Mountain, I'll either stop that or change my plan depending on the price of the various coffees that I buy.I Love my coffee.

title: Makes a great iced latte. 4 stars⭐
text:
My Keurig Elite doesn't make enough cold coffee on the iced coffee setting for me. So, I take these and make an 8 Oz. insulated mug of strong brewed coffee at night, add a little sugar, put the lid on it, and put it in the fridge. In the morning, I add a bit milk and flavored creamer and it tastes great! It tastes much like a latte.
These Green Mountain Coffee Roasters Brew Over Ice K-Cups also make a great cup of hot coffee. With a little flavored creamer and sugar, it's so easy to create whatever taste you like. I love the strong bold flavor of the coffee, as most coffees are too weak brewed in the Keurig. This one is strong and bold, but not bitter. They're a bit expensive, but they taste great!

title: Good but not great coffee; pods themselves perform well in Nespresso machine
text:
While I would not consider these to be my favorite pods that I've used in my Nespresso, it's a very drinkable brew. The flavor is strong and lively with a pleasant bite to it. What I don't like is the sourish aftertaste that is fairly pronounced. It's the type of coffee that is generally fine, but doesn't leave you craving a second cup.

The pods themselves work fine in my machine. I was a bit concerned about them cracking or leaking since the pod is plastic, but so far no problems at all with the pod fit and performance.

While the taste is not up to what I feel are Nespresso standards, the cost per pod is a lot nicer than the brand name ones. Five stars for value, three stars for taste, overall rating of 4 stars.

title: Smooth, rich flavor
text:
These pods make a cup that really hits the spot for me. Very rich and flavorful without any significant bitterness or sour aftertaste. Just perfect as-is without any milk, sugar or cream.

The pods all arrived in perfect condition, and are about 7 months away from their best-by date. There won't be any problems using all 50 in that time period! These pods are for the original line of machines.

I've tried several of the Nespresso flavors and, while I've yet to find one that I didn't care for, these are in my top three of favorite flavors. A very enjoyable cup!

title: Not at all memorable
text: This is an adequate decaf K-cup. While it lacks any significant defects, it also lacks much body or richness. The main downside is a slight sour aftertaste.  I'll certainly drink what I have, but won't be ordering it again. Better decaf K-cups can be found, such as Donut House or Timothy's Columbian decaf.

title: Good Coffee Pods
text: These pods deliver a medium roast coffee flavor.  I prefer a stronger roast but am giving them 5 stars because they delivers what the product page says - medium roast.  Hubs prefer these over dark roast pods so he definitely says they are 5 star pods.  They work well in my Nespresso machine.

title: Good Coffee Pods
text: These coffee pods already have almost 8,000 reviews.  There is nothing I could say that would be helpful or unique.  I think the coffee tastes good for being half-caffeine and they work well in my machine.

I have a Keurig at home, but I usually add my own coffee to a reusable filter basket because it's more affordable. It takes more time & I have to do a little clean up afterward, which I'm not always in the mood to do. I found the Crave Coffee online & decided to buy it because I like it & it was affordable. title: The taste is taking a little to get used to for me
text:
UPDATE -- November 29

Finally figured out how to fix this coffee so that I can enjoy it. Normally I brew 8 oz and that works fine. But I discovered, after some experimenting, that I have to brew 12 oz which seems to smooth out the taste and let the coffee flavor come through.

UPDATE -- October 31st

Been trying to drink this now for the last two weeks as my morning coffee. Still not working for me as I can't get past the taste.

Can't decide if I should just bite the bullet and send it back. Really like my Green Mountain, several flavors, and this just does not compare.
---

title: Prefer to International Delight
text:
I have used International Delight's (ID) hazelnut coffee pods for years but balked at the fact that they forced me to use more creamer than I wanted (how do you save a 1/2 pod at work) and the cheapest price I could find was at Publix.

I bought this huge pump bottle of Coffee Mate hazelnut creamer at Sam's Club for less than $15 and figured I'd just put it out at the communal coffee pot if I didn't like it.

Turns out I actually like the creamer better than ID's and one pump of the stuff is the perfect amount, less white and less sweet than a full pod of ID's. I also like that it has less than half the saturated fat of ID's creamer and I'm using less. If it truly provides 300 pumps, it'll also cost me 1/2 as much as the individual pods.

The only reason I'm not giving it 5 stars is that it really can't compete with the taste of real cream.

title: You're gonna need more than one...
text:
If you love your creamy, flavored coffee expect to open at least three every time.
As far as the flavors go, of the two offered (the original is simply an unflavored creamer) Hazelnut is the strongest. I'ts quite tasty and goes the furthest - only needing three for a 14 oz, tumbler. The French Vanilla is the next strongest which could use about 4 or 5 for the same size tumbler.
It just seems that if you enjoy very creamy coffee, like the kind you can get from the refrigerated flavored bottles, this comes in a distant second. Also, if you enjoy the sweetness that the refrigerated creamers bring you'll need to carry your own sugar / sweetener as well.
On the other hand, since they're shelf-stable they will travel if protected in a box as the plastic and foil top are quite thin. Just be prepared to stack up the empty containers as you open them to put them in your coffee.

title: Great coffee, but be sure to have a good coffee maker and use the right grind and concentration
text:
Very impressed with this coffee so far. It has a smooth full-bodied, classic coffee flavor. Smells great while it's brewing too!

Here's my recipe:
5 grams/cup (5 oz cups)
Medium grind
Bottled water
Bonavita BV1800

Note, this coffee/water ratio is about 1 tablespoon/cup. I found the strength to be just about right with this ratio... with other coffees I usually do 2 tablespoons/cup, but I have a feeling this coffee would be too strong using that amount.

title: Burnt rubber smell/taste
text:
I was looking forward to this coffee but the moment I opened the bag, my hesitations rose. Who doesn't enjoy opening a fresh bag of coffee and taking a long, deep smell. Well, from the get go this coffee had a weird chemical smell. Brewed a fresh pot and the kitchen was permeated with a bunt rubber smell (not that nice coffee aroma). Took a sip, same burnt rubber taste that lingered for a while. When my wife came down to the kitchen, her first question was "Is something burning?" . Cleaned the pot/coffee maker ran my regular coffee and there was no burnt rubber taste so I know it wasn't the coffee maker. Very disappointed.

Addendum: I was reading through some of the reviews and one review made by Christina stated that she received an "unauthentic bag".
The bag I received looks like the one she is calling the unauthentic bag so I'm hoping Amazon is willing to exchange it and the coffee is really not this bad.

Wow, I figured I would give it a try on alternate water/ground/ground fineness on my burr grinder but no luck. I resorted to making cold brew because sometimes the bitter is not captured  as much; no such luck, the burnt dirt flavor came thru. I really wanted to save this from the trashcan by trying since it is a huge 2 lb bag. Amazon says I cannot return because I didn't buy it. Amazon also says mil cannot return it. So, I will be grinding it up and tossing into the veggie garden as a soil enhancer. I have no idea how this coffee could get more than a 1-star rating, it's not even dishwater flavor diner-grade coffee.

Refrigerated
Calories 35
Saturated Fat 0
Monosaturated 19
Carbs (Sugar) 5 grams

Non Refrigerated
Calories 25
Saturated Fat 1.5 grams
Monosaturated 0

title: Great for some things, but not for others
text:
First, be aware that Erythritol will kill a dog or cat, so always protect them from it. This product is finer than regular sugar, so the dust in the air while pouring it from the bag was a concern. I had to make sure I wiped down the floor and counters in the surrounding area.

The description says you can substitute it 1 to 1 for sugar. I found that didn't work out with my snickerdoodle cookies. The dough was dry and crumbly, making me think the fineness of the sugar absorbs more oil (butter/shortening) than regular sugar. Also it did make a sweeter cookie than sugar.

It would probably be great for cinnamon sugar on toast, sweetener for cereal, oatmeal, etc. But it was a challenge to bake with. I would need to work with it more, and it's too expensive to keep testing. Maybe the company has some recipes already formulated that I'm missing. I'm undecided whether I'll buy it again, or just stick with my Pyure that I use to sweeten things.

The coffee itself tastes great, however, does not produce a full cup of coffee. We have the smaller Keurig K10 device and even though we fill standard coffee cups up to nearly the top of the cup (or about the same as 8oz internal fill line), the amount coffee in the cup after the brewing has finished is only about half of what we put in. We expected the amount of coffee to be a little less since water is in the used K-Cup, but not almost half the glass.

We thought it was the Keurig machine, however, no other brands of K-Cups have produced this issued. On our second box in the package and same issue as the first box. Of course we could add more water to make up for the loss but should not have to in my opinion.

Maybe our issue is common with this brand. If so then disappointed.

title: The Real Deal!
text:
If you live in Wisconsin, and have been to one of the stand-alone Starbucks lately, you’ve probably encountered a problem with your favorite syrup unavailable. The stores attached to Target and other retailers don’t seem to have the same problem…Strange! The staff are unable explain why it’s readily available here on Amazon, but obviously they don’t make it.

I tried the other common brand that’s available at many if our local grocery stores, but I wouldn’t recommend it, and suggest you buy the Starbucks branded syrup that’s available here. You can still have them make espresso or a latte without flavor, and add the syrup yourself (six pumps if you buy the little screw on pump that fits three bottles!). Word…

title: Very Smooth
text:
I am a big coffee drinker and my tastes were becoming expensive. I wanted an organic coffee and my husband wanted a cheap coffee. I found this bulk bag and was hesitent at first. I have never come across true organic coffee this affordable. I am glad I gave it a try; it is a pretty good coffee for the price. I ordered the medium/dark roast and it is perfect. I like my coffee black, so I need a fairly good coffee or I can't drink it. My husband on the other hand uses so much creamer that he can get waffle house coffee and be fine. I like the fact this is whole bean, it makes the morning coffee so fresh, unlike pre ground coffees, which lose their flavor shortly after opening. I will probably re-order for our cheap every day coffee.

Update
I wanted to add that after cold brewing this in my French press it brought out a nice chocolate flavor that was hardly there before. If you have a French press, I strongly suggest trying a cold brew of this coffee. To do a cold brew you grind it roughly, don't make it fine, and toss it in there with cold water and give it a stir and cover for 12 hours. I always do mine in the evening so I wake up when it is ready.

title: Strong vanilla flavor, if that's what you want!
text: I was looking for a creamer to go with Oregon Chai (or teas in general).  At the time I did the initial search, I didn't see any 'plain' Coffee-Mates on sale, so tried this one since I like both cinnamon and vanilla.  Unfortunately, these creamers deliver TOO much vanilla flavor!  It's hard to over-power the spice-tea-plus-chai blend I prefer, but this does....  I'd still recommend it for someone who brews fairly bland tea and wants to inject some vanilla (and a bit of cinnamon) for a taste change (so you don't mind that it predominates); OR if you brew your tea really strong and need a strongly flavored creamer to taste the creamer at all.  Not being a coffee-drinker, I can imagine that this creamer might be just fine in coffee - the strong flavor might be needed to punch through the java....  But, I'm switching to plain unflavored Coffee-Mate, myself.

title: Price is right on this pikes place - keep it in the pantry just in case
text:
I like light to medium roasts better these days. Better than dark roast. Columbia is a Starbucks instant coffee that was my favorite for several years - it has little more depth of flavor. And is nice in the winter’s cold blustery weather.
As my taste lightens, I like this Pikes Place well enough too. It’s at a nice price here. Very convenient packaging.
I just read that the process to make instant coffee makes it considerably higher in acrymide I think it is - a big cancer causer. So, I hope to get back to buying fresh whole beans. Grinding it myself for French press or drip coffee again, soon. I hope. Tastes better than instant, anyway. Just takes a couple of minutes each morning, to pamper myself that way.
Kenya is a very good Starbucks bean. Starbucks profiles Kenya as “juicy” - and when I taste it - well it is : ) ! Ethiopian Harrar is an excellent flavorful bean. When you find it fresh. The profile for Harrar is “like red wine” and that too seems right on the money. Haven’t found a great Harrar on Amazon. Hope they find a roaster who has a good quality, very fresh Harrar bean consistently.

title: Yummy! But not cost-effective.
text:
Like printer ink, Nespresso makes their big bucks on their coffee pods. That said, the Nespresso Variety Pack is very tasty with an intense robust flavor that you may want to dilute with sugar and/or milk. I enjoy all five flavors included in this Variety Pack and snork down six shots a day. I read some posts from people having problems with crushed or old coffee pods, but my order arrived in two days in perfect condition.

Like all coffee pods, the Nespresso pods are not very cost-effective or environmentally friendly. So I also bought four Sealpod stainless steel pods and load my own Espresso Marco Polo from the coffee store. It's a bit more trouble, but it cuts down on the unit cost and lets me enjoy my favorite cup o' Joe.

I knew what these cost when I bought these and will buy the Nespresso Variety Pack again. But I will also continue using the Sealpod units. Five stars.

text:
I have always used the refrigerated hazelnut liquid creamers, and I tried to switch to this pump cleamer to reduce calories. However, this product was not for me.

Flavor: it was too sweet. I prefer the refrigerated creamer. And if I only used 1 pump it barely turned my coffee color, so I had to use 2 pumps, which made it too sweet.

Pump: I read all the directions, and went to prime the pump. I was on about 50 pumps and it still didnt come out. So I stopped counting and just continued to pump. Finally after several minutes the stream came. Idk if I got a defective pump but one single pump did not measure out to the serving size it said on the packaging.

I will not be buying this again.. it wasn't for me.

text: I ordered this for my husband who is the resident coffee snob at our house. He isn't a fan of regular Folgers coffee, but he does like lighter roasts, so I figured it was worth a try. It's quite a bit of coffee for the price. Unfortunately though he really wasn't a fan. He said it's way too bold for a light roast and it "Tastes like the coffee you'd find for free at a bank or in a church foyer. Not GOOD coffee." So there you have it. I guess we won't be ordering this one again.

title: Just Kinda *Meh*
text: I ordered this for my coffee snob husband. When I asked him what he thought of it, he said, "Meh. It was ok... But not something I would order again. I don't have to choke it down, but it's just not anything really that special. It's kind of like the coffee you'd expect to find in the lobby at a church or a bank. Ok, but not fantastic." So there you have it. We'll finish it, but wasn't really a do-again for us.

title: What Magic is This?!?!
text: I'm very solidly into real adulthood now and always prided myself on the fact that I'm not "Coffee Dependent" like some of my friends. I do enjoy a good cup and the caffeine boost is great and all, but up until now I just haven't met a coffee that I just couldn't start my day without. Guys. I believe I may have met my match. I ordered this for my husband who is the resident "Coffee snob" at our house, but figured I'd give it a shot since it smelled so delicious as he was brewing it. I brewed a cup on the 6oz setting over ice and added coffee creamer, took a sip and had that moment like you see in movies with fireworks and rainbows and butterflies and a chorus singing in the background. If my husband wants any more of this stuff he's gonna have to fight me for it. Oh. My. Word. This stuff gives any major coffee chain I've ever tried a run for their money, and I don't have to sit behind 10 cars in the drive through and it costs literally pennies to make. I'm a huge fan and can see this being the gateway to slipping into coffee snobbery myself. I'd give it 10 stars if I could.

title: Good, Basic Cup O' Joe.
text: Coffee and Vitamins? What will they think of next? I just had to try this coffee and vitamin blend! The coffee isn't the best I've ever had, but it's a good, basic, medium roast cup o' joe. The flavor isn't super exciting and it didn't really blow my socks off, but it's not bad. I like the fact that there are B vitamins included... So that makes this a health drink, right? The coffee I received was fresh and good quality. I would order this again if it was a good deal, but otherwise I think I'd try one of the flavored varieties next time.

title: Nice Brew; No Acid Problem
text:
I have been enjoying this organic coffee for a few weeks now. When it first arrived, I was concerned how it seemed not to be sealed; I could squeeze air out of the sealed bag. I notified vendor and got an almost immediate reply that it was natural to be able to force air out as the beans were expelling CO2. Made sense; had never had this happen with bags of whole beans I bought in stores although with a slight squeeze, I could faintly smell the delicious coffee fragrance.
When I decided to research coffees, I was looking for something that had low acid as this had become an issue for me recently. I could not drink a whole cup of coffee without my stomach complainng, feeling nauseous.
Very happy to report the acid stomach has stopped-----BUT I am now using a cold brew coffeemaker and I understand that alone could have resolved the acid stomach problem. Still, I like the flavor and mellowness of the Subtle Earth Organic Coffee and felt the price was excellent.

title: Starbucks Joins The Tasteless K-Cups Crowd
text:
I have had Starbucks K-Cups before and they were reasonably good. This current batch has all the flavor and the taste of tepid
dishwater. In a recent review I lumped Starbucks in with some of the better K-Cup providers such as Peet's and Coffee People. That was based on a previous experience with Starbucks. It no longer belongs with this higher rated group but falls into the pit with the vast majority of K-Cup providers whose only redeeming value is that K-Cups are convenient to use even though they are weak and tasteless.

If you like a mediocre cup of coffee then these Starbucks will meet your requirements. If you like a bit of a kick and like at least as much taste as a K-Cup can provide then give Peet's or Coffee People a try. If all else fails and you want a cup of real coffee get yourself a grind and brew coffee maker and a bag of coffee beans.

title: An interesting new Erythritol+Steviol Glucoside Zero Calorie Sweetener
text: Purecane seems to be Erythritol (a sugar alcohol) with "Reb M". I will assume "Reb" stands for Rebaudioside and "M" seems to be the mystery. Nothing on the Purecane website explains either, but they do suggest that Purecane has one or more Rebaudiosides in it just like Stevia. Wikipedia refers to Rebaudiosides A-E as the steviol glycosides made by Stevia plants, but I cannot find "Reb M" anywhere. Perhaps it is just Purecane's name for whichever steviol glycoside is made by the Cargill GMO yeast used to derive their "Eversweet" brand of steviol glycoside from sugarcane. So Eversweet=Reb M? And Purecane=Erythritol+Eversweet? Guessing here. Note that Purecane itself is Non GMO. The yeast used to make the Reb M is GMO, but we must assume none of the yeast gets into the Purecane. Perhaps the Purecane folks will clarify all of this on their web site eventually? Other than all the above, Purecane tastes great and I will continue to use it along with my regular Stevia extract and Monkfruit.

text: Quality control is the keyword here.  These two batches are different, they are not low acid.  I am about to get a test kit and test them out.  The roast even tastes different.  It even made me wonder if this legit or not.  I hate getting a product I put trust in that is completely the opposite of what I am expecting.  It's not like this coffee is cheap.  And if you have stomach issues and problems with acid this is actually a big deal.  I didn't want it to be the coffee, but it definitely is, especially after hearing my room-mate is having issues with it, we checked and it's the new batches we bought.  Two bags from the same batch, different taste, maybe even a different bean, with who know what else??!  Hopefully this isn't counterfeit.  They have lost my trust, I want answers!  And I want my money back.  Such a disappointment, now I am on the search for alternatives.  Luckily I just ordered another brand, (Tyler's Coffee, Low Acid), maybe they will hold up to their claims.  I wondered if it would be different in larger batches, or just in general online...  what gives Pureroast???

100% Colombian blend: okay
French Roast: weird taste

Added a week later: The French Roast is really weird tasting. I mean--really weird. All flavors are too weak on the 12 oz setting but okay on the 8 oz setting (except for the French Roast).

Because of the great price, I think most of us really, really want to like this coffee. In some cases I think we fool ourselves into rating this coffee higher than it deserves (including me). But as much as I loved the price, I don't think I would buy this again.

So either sell less and bring the price down or place the 40 pods in a bigger box, because somethings got to give here. Luckily I only had 1 that was partially open, but usable (I used this opened one as my water amount tester). Two of my pods were a little crushed, but I was able to shape it back into its natural form. If you use 8 ounces of water the flavor is on point. Don't use 6 ounces of water it will leave a lot of Hot Chocolate Mix inside the pods after its done spewing your Hot Cocoa. Also, don't use 10 ounces, it'll be more of a muted flavor. title: Good value, but not a ton of flavor in these blends.
text:
Variety is the spice of life, and so rotating coffees from around the world is a fun way to start the day. In this pack, you get a whopping 96 pods from four different regions. There's the Brazil Primo, the Kenya Supreme, the Kona Blend, and a variety from Papua New Guinea. They are all make a decent cup of coffee for a K-Cup, but I can't say that any especially stand out of the pack.

My favorite, the Brazil Primo seems to have the most flavor, with some slight burnt and crusty notes (which I enjoy). And it seems to be slightly more bitter than the rest, though none of them are overpowering. The Papua New Guinea blend is my second favorite, as it's a bit darker than the others but has a very clean taste. Tied in 3rd are the Kenya and Kona blends, which aren't very bitter, not particularly dark, and don't have much flavor.

In general, these are all decent cups of coffee. You might not even notice the difference between any of the blends, especially if you're pouring this into a to-go container as you're rushing out the door to work (as I often am). You get a lot of coffee for the price, just don't expect these to knock your socks off with flavor.

title: Makes good espresso, french press
text:
I first tried Cafe Don Pablo's medium-dark roast (whole bean). I did not care for the flavor profile on the medium-dark roast (see my review on that one if you want to know why). I decided to give the light roast a try and that is now on my monthly purchasing cycle (since April/March 2017). The roast itself is almost medium. Beans are uniform. There are very few split, broken, or defective beans. I've seen only a few (if any) quakers, sour, or floater beans.Very few husks if any. So far with all my purchases I have not found any stones sticks or twigs. Finally, I have not seen any black or partially black beans.
The General Flavor Profile: light milk chocolate with a hint of roasted almonds, nutmeg, and charred oak.
Makes good espresso, french press, and kettle coffee. For some people the flavor profile might be a bit light for drip coffee or pour-over.
While I cannot say "I love it", it is a very good standard multi-use coffee at a price point of about $8 - $8.50 per pound.

title: The good: Price Grinds well Few if any bad beans ...
text:
Before my formal review, attached are pics of the product brewed for espresso. The pic with the three different roasts has the product, Subtle Earth Coffee med-dark roast, on the left. Center bottom is a light roast and on the right is what I would call a med-dark roast. I also have a pic with the coffee ground for espresso. I use a Classic Gaggia with filtered water.
The good:
Price
Grinds well
Few if any bad beans

Next "your preference":
Very smoky in oder & taste
Strong tobacco flavor
Chard oak flavor
Bitter stale dark chocolate

The bad:
There is no nuance or subtlety
This is really a DARK roast
The tobacco smoky taste is over the top
The bean should not be roasted this dark.
I would not recommend this to coffee oficionados.
I would recommend it to smokers that drink coffee or to those that love smoky tobacco flavors.

text:
Starbuck's Pike Roast is my favorite coffee. I ordered 96, which was 4 boxes of 24, as described.

All the pods I have used have worked perfectly, but I have never had a pod burst in my Keurig. I read somewhere to push down and puncture the bottom first, then put the lever down that punctures the pods. I don't always remember to do that, but I have never had a pod burst, so it must work.

My pods were in 4 Starbuck's boxes, packed in a box with the Starbuck's logo. The expiration date is March, 2022.

I read all the reviews and saw the complaints, so I tried to address the complaints against my experience. With the price being $39.97, I am probably going to order 96 more before the price increases.

Hopefully if you order this, you'll get the same buyer experience that I had.

If you're looking for mainly medium blends, this is a good selection at a great price.

I am not a coffee expert. My favorite coffees, so far, are PEETS' Cafe Domingo and Starbucks' Pike Place Roast. I also like the McCafe Premium Roast Medium.

I am a relatively new Keurig user, so right now I am just having a blast trying different coffees. I always thought that I liked strong coffee, but am finding that I prefer more of a medium to medium dark roast.

Nothing in this selection is undrinkable. Some are just better than others. For some reason, I thought French Roast was included in this mix, but it wasn't, so I ordered a box of French Roast to try and will review that separately.

When I received the box, a couple of the corners were wet, which I thought might have been due to the weather, but when I opened it up I found about 10 of the containers had opened up in transit.  I washed all the other containers off, let them dry for a few days, and put them into the bins we store them in.  Over the next three months, a few more opened up and started to leak.  This time mold formed on the other ones and we ended up having to throw most of them away.  I have not had this problem with the smaller boxes, so I will go back to them from this point forward.

title: Love it
text:
My wife loves Coffee-mate, but the powder version is a mess and the dairy version not only has a shelf life but has to be refrigerated. My wife objects to the refrigeration on two levels: 1) it takes up space in the re-frig and it's the most expensive version of CoffeMate and 2) it cools the hot drink you are trying to make.

These mini-cups are stored in individual units at room temperature, so no refrigeration and no cooling down of the hot drink you are making. I haven't compared with the cost of powder, the little cups probably are more per application, but having no mess is worth it. Users of the mini-cups are able to pour just the amount they need.

title: Tasty Iced Coffee
text: Green Mountain's brew over Ice vanilla caramel is a delicious entry into the K-Cup field.  It's made on the stronger side so it is bold enough to have the flavors come through when iced down.  While it is designed to be brewed right over ice I found that it is better to brew into a cup and then add ice after the coffee has cooled down a bit.  It helps prevent ice melting and watering down the coffee before it gets cold.  The vanilla and caramel notes shine through and give the brew just the right amount of sweetness.  Overall this is an excellent choice for iced coffee lovers.

It has a mid century modern feel with the flower prints and is a springy teal color that brings the brand's signature color into full effect.  The standout coffee flavor is the coconut mocha.  It has a nice balance between the sweet coconut and the rich mocha.  It is an excellent addition to their duos line.  This is a great gift to give to someone special or to treat yourself.
