title: Good coffee; Great price;  Stupendous combination
text:
Coffee tastes are pretty individual, but I like this, and love the price. Buying coffee has always been like throwing darts to me -- I don't know much about what all the "terms" mean, dark, french, light, etc. I'd just add flavored sweeteners and cream to make it taste good. When I bought the french press a year ago, I decided to try to like a coffee without all the doctoring; to figure out the mystery of how anyone could like black coffee. Okay, I still don't like it black. I, in fact, prepare it "bulletproof" style. But I'm not adding nearly as much sweetener, which was a prime goal.

This coffee is rich and not bitter to me. I do use more of it than other coffees I've tried, but that's a small price to pay. (I use three heaping scoops for about 12 ounces of water.) My last coffee tryout was the Sumatra blend, which also got very high scores. It was good also. I like this just a little better because I think it tastes slightly richer. And, it's available on Subscribe and Save, which I use and it saves even more money. Definitely worth a shot to see if it floats your boat :-)

title: Better than I expected

title: this batch of coffee tastes like a dirty ashtray smells
text: I have been using Keurig coffee makers for years. We always have one at home and we use them at work. We use it for hot and iced drinks. Even the kids use it for an acai berry juice drink. In the past we have purchased Green Mountain Coffee K-Cups so I have some room for comparison with this particular coffee and we are regular drinkers of other brands of Sumatran coffee. Okay, all that being said . . . this batch of coffee tastes like a dirty ashtray smells. As a former smoker I can assure you, I know exactly what ashtrays smell like and this coffee really truly tastes like a dirty ashtray. Just to be sure it was not the unit itself I brewed tea and made a juice drink which tasted great. So, this is either really old stock is being sold on Amazon or Green Mountain have changed what they are putting in the Sumatran K-cups. And I have 144 of them. Yay me.

title: Great flavor
text: The price could be better. Yes, $0.7 per shot (double/lungo) is cheaper than going to a coffee shop but it's not really all that reasonable. But the convenience is great, the sealed pods mean the flavor stays legit, the coffee is not triggering my reflux (which even some decafs do due to acidity) and you can get recycling bags for free from nespresso to ship back your used pods and not just toss them in the landfill. title: Love these coffees
text: Fantastic product for the cost.  I had a subscription for a K-cup service at a much higher cost and their assortment boxes turned out to be 12 of the same flavor with a few special flavors thrown in.  This product is different.  Sure there are duplicates, who wants just one of their favorites, but there are only a few of each with a huge variety of great flavors that compliment each other.  Another problem with some flavored k-cups is that they taste like cheap coffee with a weak hint of flavor as an after thought.  These are different and delicious.  The special flavors are predominately present and the coffee base is strong and flavorful.  Whether you are looking for a weekend relaxing cup or one every day to jump start you on your way to work these coffees are the way to go.  Delicious and the price is great for the quality.  I have not tried the chocolate peanut butter one but my wife loves those.  I love the chocolate orange and the amaretto.  Such a variety.  Definitely worth trying at least once.  You will not regret it.

title: Read this if you accidentally screwed on the pumping mechanism before activating the pump---this is a success story!
text:
As many others have reviewed, there is an unfortunate lack of directions in how to install the pumping mechanism. I made the mistake others made. However, a strong young man came to my aid and was able to serendipitously fix the pump spout so that it worked. You aren't supposed to screw the pump spout in before unlocking the pumping mechanism. I did not read reviews before purchasing, because I knew I liked this flavor, and I was getting a super good deal. The other reviews were helpful when I had screwed in the pumping mechanism without activating it first and then I needed to figure out how to fix it.

Other reviewers unfortunately had to throw out their whole bottle of creamer when they screwed in the pump without activating it first. If you someone around who is very strong, have them try to unscrew it. That is how we accidentally got the one I ordered to work!

Next, I took 2 kcups, opened them, and packed the coffee in my espresso maker to extract as much flavor as possible. The brewed coffee was transparent, very weak. The flavor was also weak. title: No refrigeration required
text:
Love the vanilla marshmallow like flavor of this creamer. It also lasts forever and doesn’t need to be kept in the refrigerator.

It will, over time, get some crystallization that clogs the pump, which wouldn’t be a big deal, except the pump is designed so that you can’t easily unscrew it after it has been put on. So when it clogs, you either have to throw it out, or, like me, get a big pair of pliers and force it to unscrew. Then just run hot water through the pump and put it back on and you’re good to go.

title: Lasts forever and doesn’t require refrigeration - can clog
text:
This is my favorite flavor of creamer and kind of tastes a bit like marshmallow. This particular option does not need to be kept in the refrigerator and lasts for a super long time, making it perfect for a home or small office environment.

I knocked a star off, because from time to time the creamer will get small lumps in it that clog the included pump. The pump screws on to the bottle with a locking mechanism, preventing it from being easily removed. So if it clogs like this, you have to use a pair of large pliers to force it to unscrew, run hot water through it to clear the clog, and then screw it back on. Which is a fairly involved process.

title: Good coffee in 9 oz. bags with vitamins (and carbs) added
text:
I am a fan of Starbucks, though I don't always spring for the cost at their coffee shops. Nevertheless, I enjoy their coffee when I get it. I was a little suspicious of this medium roast coffee with essential vitamins added. However I was pleased with this coffee.

I really need to add biotin to my diet at this time and what more painless way to take it than in my Starbucks! You won't notice any additions, but if you are a carb counter, do notice that a 12 oz. cup of this coffee has 20 calories, 3 g of carbs and 1 g of protein. I don't like this quite as well and their usual Pike coffee or as much as Café Verona, but it brews a respectable, even good cup of coffee. Note that these packages are 9 oz. each (getting smaller all the time, but isn't that the way the story goes).

Note: Since I started drinking this coffee about a month ago, my nails have stopped splitting--so it does contain enough biotin to make a difference--I wonder if I can buy Starbucks with my FSA health account ;).

title: Stronger than most flavored coffees. Nice taste.
text:
I cannot get through a day without having at least 4 coffee's, usually more. For the most part I don't usually go for flavored coffee but thought I'd give this a try, after all I love white chocolate. Most flavored coffee is usually fairly weak in strength which is why I tend not to go for them.

Donut Shop White Chocolate Vanilla is stronger than most flavored coffees making it ideal for me. You can taste the subtle hint of white chocolate but it doesn't overwhelm you in any way, not so much with the vanilla. I couldn't taste any hint of vanilla, perhaps the white chocolate covered that. Strangely for a flavored coffee, this wasn't sweet at all, infact it came across as slightly bitter.

The package I received contained 4 boxes each with 24 K-Cups inside, still giving you a 96 count, rather than a single larger box of 96.

Overall it produces it a nice fairly stout mug of coffee with a hint of white chocolate.

title: Smooth and flavorful.
text:
I am a huge coffee drinker, going through about 2 pots of coffee per day. Recently I got a Keureg Duo allowing me to not only make pots of coffee using standard ground beans but can now also just get a single mug whenever I want. I love my new coffee maker however as I'm new to Keurug cups, I've been on the searching for good blend that I like. Mostly I like the taste of breakfast blends and have been trying out a few including the starbucks blend and donut shop blends amongst others.

Timothy's is light roast breakfast blend. I tend to prefer light roast over medium or dark blends as it doesn't leave that aftertaste. Timothy's is a smooth flavorful blend with no aftertaste. A slightly sweeter blend of coffee than most others I've tried which tend to be a little on the bitter side. It reminds me quite a bit of the Maxwell house breakfast blend however it has more aroma and a fuller body than Maxwell house.

If you like breakfast blend coffee or are looking for a smooth flavorful cup of coffee then Timothy's is the one to go for. Of all the different K-Cups that I've tried, this is the one I'm sticking with.

title: at best. I usually go with Eight 'O Clock (which ...
text: Marginal, at best. I usually go with Eight 'O Clock (which is a good, reasonably priced whole bean coffee), but thought I'd give this a try after reading all the positive reviews...which is becoming a problem with Amazon. I've purchased a couple of products lately that have had a large number of rave reviews, but turned out to be like this: marginal at best. And after researching further into the "positive" reviews, many appear more than a little suspicious. Anybody who wants to can review a product on Amazon without any proof that they actually used the product. Amazon really needs to limit reviews to people who actually purchased the product from Amazon. Don't waste your money on this coffee. (And, yes, I realize that opinions are like...well, you know. So, take it for what it's worth.)

title: Low Acid, Low Taste, High Price
text: I hate leaving bad reviews for anything. Out of 85 reviews I’ve written here to date, this is only my second one-star review, but I think it is warranted. I have a confirmed medical condition that flares up from time to time, necessitating the need for me to drink low-acid coffee for at least a few months, or until I can get the symptoms under control. I only drink one cup of coffee a day, in the morning, so that one cup has to really count. I’ve used the Puroast ground coffee many times in the past, in the French Roast and House Blend varieties, and it has always been fine, if not quite as good as “normal” coffee. This time, however, it was awful; it smells and tastes like burnt chemicals, and not at all like coffee.  There is also no expiration date anywhere on the package. I am not a coffee snob, although I appreciate a good cup, but this is undrinkable as far as I’m concerned.  PLEASE NOTE: Even if you order this through Amazon Prime, they will not offer a refund of any kind, so buyer-beware.

It is a nice balance of medium to dark roast in both it's flavor and it's effectiveness after cup #1.

It brews up well in the Keurig. There haven't been any grounds in our cups and the flavor isn't plastic tasting like you sometimes get from other cups.

I would recommend having a place to store all these cups when they arrive. They come in a fairly large box and are just put in there. You will need to make some closet space or have a drawer for each of them.

Great cup of coffee. Don't be scared off too much if you don't care for medium roasts, this one is on the heavier side and worth a try.

title: Like the Flavor
text:
I've never tried this brand before so I don't have any comparison. This has an interesting taste with the brewed guayusa, which is something I've never tried before. Apparently the leaves come from the Amazon and I've never heard of them before trying this drink. It's a little bit like the flavor of tea without the bitterness.

I really like the flavor of the passion fruit. This is like a sweet tea drink, which I like. The sweetness comes from a mix of organic cane sugar and stevia. The added stevia keeps the calorie content down while adding sweetness. However, i think it's the stevia that gives it a little bit of an aftertaste I'm not wild about. Stevia is one of my least favorite sweeteners. Luckily. it doesn't completely ruin the drink. Overall, I like this. I don't love it. I like that the caffeine comes from a natural source.

title: Take THAT finicky stomach!
text:
My inlaws are from New England. Which means they're coffee addicts, naturally. This is the land that brought you coffee syrup to put in milk, mind you. When I discovered that recent health issues forced my mom-in-law to cease drinking coffee due to pain, it saddened me. Caffeine blasphemy is a terrible, terrible crime in Rhode Island.

Thankfully the good people at Puroast came to the rescue. For the first time in ages, coffee no longer hurt adopted mama. The flavor distinctive enough to mesh with morning breakfast. Dare I say it...a second pot followed the first.

Sure, there's monkey picked beans, top notch Kona, and better options out there. All moot points if you can't drink any of 'em. For coffee lovers with a rebellious digestive system, here's your passport back to a beloved caffeine buzz.

title: Not The Best, But Much More Affordable Than Nespresso
text: At first, I was not that impressed with the flavor of these Ilan’s Nespresso Compatible Premium Gourmet Espresso Medium Roast Pods. Then, after being away from my Nespreso machine for over a week and drinking only regular drip coffee, I tried the Ilan Pods again and was very happy with the taste. I had to admit that I had become completely spoiled by my Nespresso machine. These Ilan’s Nespresso Compatible Pods may never taste quite as good as the originals, but they are still pretty darn good for a cup of cafe quality espresso coffee that I can make at home. Plus, the price for a 60 Count order of the Ilan Pods would barely get me half the number of Nespresso pods. I can't argue with the affordability of these Ilan Pods given that the taste of the coffee is still so good, but just not the best.

The value here at 30 some cents per pod is excellent, and the coffee itself is very drinkable, with no particular off (sour or bitter) flavors that some coffees can have. This pack of coffees comes in four varieties: Italian Roast, Caramel Macchiato, Seattle Dark and Kona Blend.  My favorite is the Seattle Dark, which I would rate at 5 stars, w the Caramel Macchiato being 3, and the Kona and Italian being 4. These are good for everyday coffee, imo, and are a solid choice and value.

title: Yum. Great Smell and Maple Flavor
text: This Crave Coffee Single, Keurig K-Cup Compatible 40 count in Vermont Maple smells and tastes wonderful. I'm not a huge fan of flavored coffees, feeling that they tend to hide substandard coffees underneath, or because they often taste oddly artificial. But this Crave Vermont Maple smells wonderful to me, and tastes very good- almost lightly sweet, while still retaining the taste of a good coffee. My husband who always drinks his coffee black, asked me whether I had added sugar to his coffee when he tried this (no sugar in here). I guess the nose can really fool the tastebuds. This is a lovely flavor, and a nice drink to savor any time of day.

Also, they were not as packed as some K cups as they are not listed as BOLD. BOLD K cups have more coffee in each cup. These tasted too weak for me but I wouldn't buy them for myself as I like a darker blend.

title: Delicious and natural tasting cocoa
text: My best friend loves chocolate more than anything and only uses k-cups for her coffee and hot beverages.  She fell hard for the taste and quality of this Junior Mints Hot Cocoa.  She is indeed the expert on quality and hates it when the taste of chocolate is artificial, overly sweet or chalky just not tasting really like chocolate.  She tasted this and right away she said she could picture sitting at a ski lodge sipping this cocoa.  The addition of the peppermint flavor with the delectable dark chocolate hit just the right note.  Also with hot cocoa not made with milk often it is watery just not providing the warmth and refreshment cocoa should.  She said it passed all those tests with high marks.  I love it as well and find this a truly original and tasty product.  I often go to it at night when not wanting caffeine but wanting a nice warm drink.  But I digress, my opinion does matter but my friend is the expert.  So with our combined opinions I highly recommend this Junior Mints Hot Cocoa Pods for Keurig K-cup brewers.  Do yourself a favor and allow yourself this special treat.  This is a product that delivers on what it advertises and is delicious to the last drop.

title: Very tasty coffee!
text:
I love this coffee!!! It's one of my favorites! I like to get a 5 lb bag of whole bean. I fill up a small coffee grinder of beans at a time, to preserve freshness. If you don't use the grounds fairly quickly, it will get a bit stale and loose it's aroma. Fresh ground coffee is always best anyway!

I love how detailed the packaging is about where the beans are sourced and how they're handled. It makes me more confident in the product. I also love that it's organic!

Try just a bit of honey in your cup! It doesn't taste right with every type of coffee, but it tastes really good with this one! I started using honey instead of sugar to be a bit healthier, and use less sweetener. Good quality honey provides extra health benefits, don't have to use as much sweetener, and tastes great! I just do black coffee with a bit of honey and it's perfect!

text:
I love flavored coffee and these were the most authentically flavored coffees I've had. Except the Hazelino Muffin. That one tasted very artificial and was not good. The caramel and the vanilla were wonderful. I repurchased the vanilla on its own. You don't save any money buying them in a bundle or from Amazon. You're better off buying individual packs straight from the Nespresso website. Plus if you sign up for the regular subscription, you get a free pack of coffee after every $75 you spend. Which you will spend quickly at these prices.

TLDR; Buy individual packs from nespresso.com instead of this bundle. This bundle doesn't save you any money and they're not all good.

title: A liquid cinnamon roll...
text: I have not yet ordered this from Amazon so this will not be a verified purchase review.  Based on a few reviews, I wasn't sure I would like it so I picked up a small box locally.  And I can report that indeed I do not like it...I LOVE it!  It has a light cinnamon flavor and an almost buttery finish.  I do add coconut cream and vanilla bean ghee to my coffee, so perhaps this is part of it, but this tastes like a liquid cinnamon roll.  I will be ordering for sure...this is one I can--and will--drink every day.  Absolutely delicious...so smooth, too.  I am not normally a flavored coffee person, but this is wonderful coffee, and I highly recommend it.

title: The Best Coffee Ever & Reasonably Priced!
text:
I like all of Victor Allen’s coffee. I drink my coffee black, so flavor is very important to me. The dark roasts (Italian & French) are very bold and smooth, and not bitter at all. The Italian has a slight hint of licorice and is delicious.

The medium (Colombian, Kona, Donut Blend & Morning Blend) are all excellent with great flavor and no bitterness whatsoever. They say the Morning Blend is a light roast, but I think it’s closer to a medium roast.

I just tried tried the Caramel Macchiato and it was wonderful! Not too heavy on flavor, just enough to compliment the coffee. It doesn’t taste like cheap flavored coffee.

I have ordered a lot of Victor Allen K-Cups and have never had any issues with brewing or damaged or empty cups. Victor Allen is hands down my favorite coffee.

title: Authentic coconut milk, excellent taste, clean ingredient
text:
Authentic coconut, Great taste, clean ingredient
I’m very sensitive with artificial flavors in food. And I used to drink fresh coconut milk. So I can tell the taste is from real coconut meat. I used Kos coconut powder to mix with match green tea latte. At first, I wasn’t so satisfied with the coconut taste in the mixture. Later on, I found out that if mix KOS coconut powder in the boiling hot water, it creates a creamy smooth flavor just exactly how a fresh one. So in the green tea latte, I froth the coconut milk power in hot water first, then add cold water to bring down the temperature before adding matcha. It creates a perfect lovely tasty healthy latte. Love it love it and love it. Highly recommended!

I already had a positive opinion of the KOS products I've tried and decided to give the Organic Coconut Milk Powder a go.

It has 15 calories and 1 Smart Point per scoop, has a nice, rich flavor, blends nicely in hot or cold coffee, and adds a little something when blended in a protein smoothie. One thing to keep in mind is that the powder can get clumpy in the tub, so make sure to break up the clumps before using it when you're going to use a frother. It takes a little longer to break up the clumps once it's added to the drink. In a blender, no biggie. I'm going to blend up a little to use in baking soon to see how well it does. I have high expectations.

I will definitely buy this again.

title: A Better Choice  for a Healthy Gluten-free, Non-Dairy Coffee/Tea Creamer
text:
Because I had become intolerant to both gluten and dairy products, I decided to try a good quality and healthy gluten-free, and non-dairy coffee creamer to use in my coffee and tea. I was impressed with the nutritive components in KOS Organic Coconut Milk Powder ingredients, and decided to give it a try.

I really enjoy the taste of coconut, so adding the powder to my coffee was a pleasant enhancement!

My only complaint is that I prefer a creamier consistency for my coffee, and, unfortunately I did not find that the coconut milk powder adds any amount of creaminess, like half and half dairy creamers deliver...especially with the suggested serving size of only 1 teeny tiny scoop, (2 grams), of the powder. I have increased that amount to two (2) scoops and, I am still not satisfied with the degree of creaminess. Aside from the lack of creaminess, I still enjoy the smooth taste of coconut flavor which is imparted by this delicious powder. I believe it is a better choice for a healthy gluten-free, non-dairy coffee/tea creamer, and will consider purchasing over and over again.

title: Highest quality available on Amazon
text: This product is exactly as promised -- unadulterated dehydrated coconut milk.  No fillers, no sweeteners, just the pure product.  Its taste is amazingly better than the product we have been using...that we thought was pretty good.  Remember that without the fillers, etc it won't mix in as easily (much better into a warm/hot liquid) and may clump up if not stored cool and dry.

title: Does size REALLY matter?
text:
If you're comparing these 2, like I did, you might think it is the same exact ingredients and opt for the larger container, but you'd be making a huge mistake. Trust me, looks are deceiving in this instance. I had to throw away the Nutricost version because it smells/tastes so bad. I couldn't even mask the bitter, chemical taste in order to use it. I tried. KOS, on the other hand, is organic and smells/tastes just like coconut!!! It is subtle, not overpowering, but it's delicious. There is even a little sweetness to it. But for those considering dietary needs, as well as finances, consider this as well.

KOS brand:

179 servings
15 calories
1g of carbs
No added sugar

vs

Nutricost brand:

60 servings
100 calories
4g of carbs
No added sugar

So what looks like far more is actually far less, even if both tasted the same. But they don't. This one is AWESOME and the other one is pretty disgusting.

Also, this blends well and even froths, if you're using it as coffee creamer. Highly recommend.

title: Previously the best coffee ever, now (third bag) worst coffee ever.
text:
UPDATE: Third purchase, this is the worst coffee ever. Perhaps I got a bad roast batch, maybe it rained too much or the quality can't keep up with demand. Whatever the problem at Don Pablo, this third bag is undrinkable to even for the least discerning pallets.

My review from the First two purchases: I have experimented with, tasted, brewed and blended coffees from all over the world much to the delight of my friends and family. Kona, Costa Rican, Peruvian, Guatemalan, Kenyan, and Ethiopian are all wonderful. I can blend them in various proportions and make them even more interesting. However, I can't make this one better. Every attempt to blend this one with any other coffee has been less satisfying than a straight cup of this coffee.
It is simply THE smoothest, richest, most satisfying coffee I've ever had.
FYI: I grind fresh and do an open pour-over with the clean, pure water for the best results.
**No guarantee of the results if you put this stuff through a 10-year old, never-cleaned, bargain-brand coffee maker using Flint, Michigan tap water. It's wonderful; not magic.

text: We were ordering our coffee from a local company, and we felt good about that.  But someone pulled me aside (I order supplies for the office) and said "Can't we get Starbucks?".  I asked the coffee drinkers in the office how they all felt about it.  They all agreed, they wanted Starbucks.  I don't drink hot coffee, I drink iced coffee (yep, you guessed it, I drink Starbucks-I get the cold brew from the dairy section or the cold brew concentrate, or one of the other variations of Starbucks cold drinks from retail), but I go along with what they want.  They wanted a dark roast, so I ordered Sumatra.  They are all very happy, and don't want me to switch.  I order four bags at a time, and it is always really fresh. You can't beat the convenience of ordering from Amazon and having it delivered right to the office.

title: Wonderful way to start each morning
text:
It always takes a bit of juggling around to find the right balance between water and ground coffee. In this case, I hit it right from the beginning: a heaping 2 T coffee measure to 9 oz water (a large mug) in my single-cup drip coffee maker. From the moment I opened the bag I was impressed. I got a sudden scent of coffee richness that was immediately tempting. It smelled just like freshly ground coffee.

With a name like “donut shop” I didn’t know what to expect. Frankly, I don’t detect any added flavoring. It’s a smooth, not bitter, not sweetened but rich coffee flavor the deepness and strength of which will depend on the ratio of coffee to water you will choose and, probably, your brewing method.

Personally, I prefer to buy my coffee in bags (either beans or ground, like this one) and bypass the convenience of pods because I need the flexibility of choosing my strength. I was not familiar with the Victor Allen brand but I was attracted by the other favorable reviews and the excellent per-unit price, despite the fact that it is sort of risky to get six packages of an unknown product. In this case, I totally lucked out with this choice!

title: Strong But Not Satisfying As Taste Isn’t Rich Or Flavorful, Bitter But Lacks Sweetness (2.5 Stars)
text:
These plastic Ilan Edge capsules are for OriginalLine Nespresso machines only (not VertuoLine Machines). These capsules are rated 9 strength, 7 body and 6 aroma. I‘ve been brewing these on my Essenza Mini, and they load fine but I’ve noticed the flow is slower than other brands I’ve been using lately such as Nespresso, Pete’s, L’OR and Woken. I can drink this Ilan espresso with my usual milk and sugar, but I’m just not crazy about the taste.

Although this Ilan espresso is strong, it doesn’t taste rich and flavorful to me. Some of my favorite brands are bitter, but the bitterness in this espresso isn’t balanced with sweetness and then described cocoa notes don’t come through. The lingering aftertaste is sour and somewhat unpleasant. It may be caused by the slower drip, but I can’t really say. Those who prefer a weaker tasting espresso may like this one, but I don’t find the taste satisfying. 2.5 stars. Made in Israel.

title: Delicious Double Shot That’s Full Flavored w/ Cocoa & Caramel Notes, Smooth & Creamy-Better Value
text:
Nespresso Double Chiaro capsules brew a double espresso shot (3.4 oz) for VertuoLine machines only. This is delicious medium roast espresso. It rates an intensity 6 and I usually favor higher intensity espressos, but Chiaro is so good it’s among my favorites. It’s not as bold and lacks the bitterness of the more intense espressos like Altissio and Diavolitto, but it’s rich and full flavored with dense and creamy body and good crema. This is a smooth and balanced espresso but not bland or weak.

Chiaro makes a delicious and satisfying latte, so I don’t even miss the premium coffee chains. When I add steamed milk and sugar (or honey or Jaggery), I taste cocoa notes with a hint of caramel that’s perfect for my morning latte. I typically use two espresso capsules that brew 1.7 ounces at a time, so this double espresso capsule for just $.15 more is a great value by comparison. I’ll definitely buy Double Chiaro espresso and trade off with my higher intensity favorites. Love it. 5 stars.

title: Results shocked me.
text:
I have heard that when coffee is brewed from fresh and correctly harvested beans, that you can reap all rewards like
that of studies that have shown multiple health benefits from drinking coffee. Well these beans must be it. I'm usually sensitive to coffee. I get the shakes, upset stomach, dizziness, etc. I normally have to eat a good meal with my coffee to prevent that. However, not only did I not experience that with this coffee, which was shocking, but I also felt happy and energized for a decent period of time without a massive crash. I thought it was just me, but then it resulted the same way for my husband, although he doesn't suffer sensitivities. We've purchased a good amount of different coffees and have never experienced this before. Also, the taste is unlike anything as well; rich and velvety is truly the best way to describe it. This stuff is definitely try-worthy, and I'd recommend it to anyone, despite my temptation to keep it all to myself.

text:

BTW don't put in refrigerator. Don't leave on top of stove like I did once; only to spend about an hour cleaning up the mess I made turning on wrong burner. The mess was 'huge' and took a long time to scrape of the cooked goo off the burner.

I've seen other coffee flavors at local gas station where one or two drops does the trick. Super concentrated stuff. If this were super concentrated, I'd give it another try. For now we are buying the real stuff.

title: Interesting and convenient
text: So I was initially hesitant about this product and had some doubts about usefulness, waste, etc. HOWEVER, I came to really appreciate the concept of the single serving cans. I use my drip coffee maker a few times a week but some days use a pod machine, or an espresso maker, or even make tea. This means that for me, the time between my opening a new bag of coffee and using it all varies wildly. The single serve cans alleviate this issue! No more having a large amount of open medium roast beans or grounds going stale! If I want to try another specialty blend, my trusty Pike's Place ground will remain fresh. These are also great for travel - I liked having my own coffee on hand during a recent night in a hotel. Overall, I Recommend this product

title: A great everyday brew!
text: This coffee makes a good daily grind, balancing economics and quality. It is smooth and full bodied without being bitter, and the beans are fresh and oily when you open the package. I store them in 2-cup glass containers in the cupboard to expose as little as possible to air and light, which keeps them oily until I finish the batch. While these beans lack the complexity of more exquisite coffees, they don't leave me disappointed at this price point. For comparison, I personally prefer this over Starbucks dark roasts because it is less harsh, although slightly less complex.

My partner and I have 1 thermos full each per day, made via french press, so you're looking at spending around $1-3 a day on coffee for one to two people. Very nice price to me.

I've narrowed down what I like in a coffee over a long period of time and this seems to fit the bill. Low acidity yet a strong bold flavor, no burnt taste, not overly fruity, just a lovely and balanced brew. I have recieved comments on how good it smells and been asked where I get it. title: Coffee wonderfulness-low acidity-creamy, milk chocolate finish
text:
Subtle Earth Organic Medium Dark Roast: This is an excellent coffee. Perfectly roasted medium dark with naturally low acidity and retains the natural sweetness of the bean. It has a creamy, milk chocolate finish which is very pleasant. This is my favorite coffee by far and the pricing of it makes it a great value.
Note: I've found medium dark to dark roasts have less acidity/caffeine and do not aggravate my reflux or arthritic pains as much as lighter roasts. Do the research on darker roast/low acid coffee and less caffeine benefits.
I brew this with a Chemex brewer. The Chemex is a superior way to brew coffee and will make any coffee taste better/smoother. The Chemex filters remove undesirable acids from the brew, leaving a pot of pure, pristine, coffee wonderfulness.

title: This is really good coffee!
text:
tl;dr - This coffee is really good, try it!
Long version - the chocolate notes really shine and this is a really bold, low acidity brew. I generally use 2 things to determine coffee freshness. The amount of gas in the bag (freshly roasted coffee gives off CO2) and the amount of static in the coffee after grinding. While this bag was really compressed, there was no static in the grounds. Honestly, I will buy this again and again. I really cannot believe how good this coffee is! Plus, water process decaf and certified organic. Win-win in my book.

title: Good low acid coffee
text: I like this coffee because I can grind it up and put a couple of tablespoons in a bottom of a large mug and fill it with near boiling water treating it like Turkish coffee and sip it over time and reheat in microwave as needed. I flavor it with liquid vanilla flavored stevia. When down to about 1/3 of a cup left, I put in a honey-vanilla chamomile tea bag and fill with hot water and heat that in the microwave and add some more stevia and enjoy a second cup before tossing the grounds in my garden. This coffee does not have the acidic bite of standard coffees you buy at the grocery store and does not irritate my empty stomach like store bought coffee we have at church. Hard to compare flavor with other coffees as I have developed a taste for the way I do it.

title: Rich and Bold Flavor
text:
As a coffee addict I found Don Pablo Organic coffee beans the best ever.

The coffee is smooth and rich tasting and seems to be low in acidity as it never has upset my stomach.
The only thing I can say about the coffee beans they tend to be oily as most darker bends can be and that might effect the coffee puck coming out of the grinder. We use a Gaigga Expresso coffee machine with automatic grinder and the machine seems to be adjusting to the oilier coffee bean.

title: Great flavor!
text:
I am no coffee connoisseur but I do love coffee. Black or with cream, morning, or afternoon. This Subtle Earth medium-dark roast coffee is great.
I prefer a medium roast. I hate the charred Starbucks and unfortunately all the Costco brands that use Starbucks as their roaster. It's too ashy tasting for me - even the medium roasted beans. This coffee is different. I was worried about the medium-dark roast being too dark for me but that it not the case. I'm really impressed with this coffee. My opinion: first taste is like a dark roast but it instantly smooths out to a complex medium roast and then ends a little sweeter. It's really fascinating that every sip is the same with the progression. I'm not sure how it's roasted to taste so complex but I love it. I have a bonavita which is a bit between a pour over and an auto drip maker. I grind to the consistency of sugar, sometimes a tiny bit courser. The bigger the pot, the courser my grind for more optimal flow time. I use just shy of 2 level tablespoons per 5 ounce cup of water. It's perfect!

title: thank you, for not screwing me over

THIS is what coffee is supposed to taste like. Thank you for not screwing us. Please MAINTAIN this level of quality, and if you do, you will absolutely maintain me as your customer for as long as you do.

I purchased both a manual coffee mill and my first bag of whole bean coffee. I'm still experimenting with the amount of coffee needed vs. the amount of time needed in the French Press I brew the grounds in. The difference in fresh ground coffee vs. store bought is outstanding. The only reason I didn't give five stars is because I have nothing to compare against as this is my very first bag of whole bean. Otherwise, I've brewed both espresso and regular coffee from the Cafe Don Pablo Gourmet beans and am finding the coffee to be smooth and velvety with complex flavors that tickle the taste buds and delights the senses. Can't wait to try other whole bean flavors!

title: I have ants now due to busted creamers!!
text:
I normally don't write reviews but decided I have to for this particular item because I now have ants in our conference room. I purchased this for my office conference room for meetings. Like a few other 1-2 star reviews, when I opened up the box I noticed they were wet. Quite a few of them had busted open in transit. The plastic on these creamers is not as sturdy as ones you'll get in the rectangular stand up boxes with a 50 count. I wasn't about to go through all of them at the time so figured they'd dry up and I could deal with it later.

For every meeting, I had to hand pick out and squeeze and shake every creamer to make sure it wasn't empty or leaking. One out of every 10 or so was either empty or leaking. Not the biggest deal until today! I was stocking the room for a meeting and noticed ANTS were crawling all over the box due to more creamers having leaked.

I will not purchase the 180+ boxes any more due to the insufficient packaging on the creamer itself. I will stick to the 50 or smaller counts which I have purchased before from office supply stores as I know they have a thicker plastic for the creamer.

title: This May Be My Coffee

I started burr grinding my coffee 25 yrs. ago because most of the store ground stuff in the plastic cans all tastes stale. For years I used the big SB brand coffee until they out priced their worth. Since then, I've shopped many, many brands and varieties of coffee looking for "my" coffee. I tried several of Don Pablo's bean varieties and was satisfied with them all and the prices were about as good as it gets these days. Then I tried Cafe Don Pablo Subtle Earth Organic Med-Dark Roast. The bags are attractive. This is a smooth full flavored coffee that is full of aroma. You can taste it when you open the bag! It's not bitter nor burned tasting like many dark roasts. Now, after drinking 24 pounds of this great coffee it may be "my" coffee as long as this family business keeps their high standards. Thank you very much!

title: Excellent Coffee
text:
One of the best , if not the best, cups of coffee I have ever brewed. When opening the bag the aroma was very fresh and "coffee-ie". To me, it is medium bodied but full flavored with absolutely no bitterness. It is very smooth and well-rounded in taste and I noticed no subtle or unique flavors, only an excellent coffee flavor.

I like that it is a medium-dark roast because a medium roast usually lacks boldness whereas a dark roast can be too overbearing with that too toasted, often bitter, taste. This comes off as being the best solution between the two.

This coffee is so good I can leave it at room temperature for hours in a tight lid jar or thermos, and after SLOWLY reheating it, it will taste just about as good as when first brewed.

The beans are a combination of 50% Honduran Peaberry/50% Guatemalan San Antonio.

And, the price is outstanding for these high quality coffee beans!

This will be my regular java from now on.
